title: centaurea phrygia subsp. bosniaca
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. bosniaca is a subspecies of thistle native to the Balkan Peninsula, characterized by its large, prickly leaves and vibrant purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Bosnia and Herzegovina, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Centaurea phrygia subsp. bosniaca include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Centaurea phrygia subsp. bosniaca can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using them as a tincture, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
